goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Hybrid BM25 + dense-vector retrieval & pgvector HNSW projection
rationale: Hybrid sparse+dense fusion is retrieval-ranking over a rebuildable vector projection; no GraphRAG/retrieval service is built (semantic-web FalkorDB projection is PLANNED), so it seeds the net-new rag-retrieval-projection packet as the simpler 2-channel precursor to RRF fusion.
cautions: LegalEase license unverified - port the alpha-weighted fusion algorithm, do not copy
keep vectors as projection not authority; attach provenance spans to returned chunks

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Declarative gov-data driver base: auth + retry + Retry-After + Redacted
rationale: The Redacted-auth-header + filterStatusOk + retryTransient + version-pinned-base wrapper is the canonical shared HttpClient driver base; no shared base package exists yet (only per-LLM-driver wrappers), so it is the auth+retry kernel for the new gov-legal-data-driver-codegen shared layer.
cautions: TalentScore is an external private project - port the wrapper pattern, do not copy
reuse the repo Effect HttpClient idioms (typed errors over try/catch)

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Citation-aware rate throttle (parse-once, charge-per-citation)
rationale: Parse-once cost-metering throttle is a driver-layer resilience concern that lands on the courtlistener driver via the shared rate-limit/cost layer; the eyecite citation parse it reuses is downstream citation-grounding work (secondary), not the core of this nugget.
cautions: courtlistener (Free Law Project) is AGPL - reimplement the throttle/metering from spec, do not copy
eyecite/HYPERSCAN tokenizer is the AGPL citation parser - keep parsing in the separate citation-grounding packet

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Deterministic court/jurisdiction reference-data assembly
rationale: Template-substitution + ordinal-expansion + parent->child field inheritance is the deterministic assembly recipe for the courts-db controlled vocabulary; no court dataset/resolver exists (ip-law-knowledge-graph only defines abstract Court/Jurisdiction nodes), so it seeds the net-new court-vocabulary-resolver, ingested via official-data-sync-foundation.
cautions: courts-db is BSD-2 (permissive, attribution) - dataset/regex vendorable with attribution, resolver logic reimplement-don't-copy
graph-shape Court/Jurisdiction stays owned by ip-law-knowledge-graph; sync plumbing by official-data-sync-foundation

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Per-matter local index schema (char-span chunks + structure tables + FTS5)
rationale: The char-span chunk + FTS5 external-content + meta-version-guard INDEX SCHEMA is the rebuildable retrieval projection over PGlite/Drizzle authority (no such projection built); its deterministic structure tables (defined_terms/section_refs/parties) are filled by the separate deterministic-doc-structure-extraction extractor (secondary).
cautions: doc-haus is MIT (safe to study/adapt)
vectors/FTS index are projection, not authority - authority + provenance spans live in PGlite/Drizzle per beep's split

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Clause-aware sectionizer + char-offset chunker with breadcrumb embeddings
rationale: The offset-preserving ~2000-char chunker sitting between @beep/md and @beep/langextract plus the breadcrumb-prefixed embedding strategy are net-new ingestion-pipeline pieces with no home; they belong to rag-retrieval-projection coordinating with langextract (its V1 owns extraction-to-span, not chunk-for-retrieval).
cautions: doc-haus is MIT (safe to study/adapt)
clause-detection overlaps deterministic-doc-structure-extraction - keep the sectionizer span-bearing and provider-neutral

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> CourtListener REST v4 token-auth client
rationale: packages/drivers/courtlistener is a bare VERSION-only skeleton; this read-only REST search client + token-vs-unauth rate-limit + result-shaping (caseName/citation/court/citeCount/snippet) is exactly the implementation the skeleton needs, owned by gov-legal-data-driver-codegen.
cautions: compare remote-MCP-vs-hand-rolled choice; beep targets a native Effect driver via runpod/acp codegen precedent (not Orval/axios)
CourtListener V4 enforces 'Authorization: Token <key>' header (not Bearer)

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Layout-aware PDF extraction + OCR-need heuristic + quality gate
rationale: Layout-preserving extraction with margin crop + skew (transformation-matrix) filtering is the deferred layout-aware PDF strategy the goal explicitly defers (engineMatrix Poppler/Docling laterRole); it extends file-processing-capability as a new OCR/PDF-diagnostics phase, not a new packet, and produces stable char spans for provenance.
cautions: doctor is BSD-2 (permissive, attribution) - adapt with attribution, do not vendor wholesale
lands as a new strategy phase; do not reopen the completed P1 vertical proof

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Layout-aware PDF extraction + OCR-need heuristic + quality gate
rationale: page_needs_ocr (empty text / '(cid:' broken fonts / annotations / images / many curves) is the cheap text-vs-OCR gate the file-processing goal defers; the OCR strategy flag + ocr-disabled skip reason already exist, so the heuristic extends them rather than rebuilding.
cautions: doctor is BSD-2 (permissive, attribution)
critical for local-first cost control - run fast extraction first, fall back to OCR only when gated

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Robust MIME/extension detection with magic-byte fallbacks
rationale: Magika + magic-byte signature fallbacks (WordPerfect/ASF/PDF/FLAC) target the file-processing Artifact MIME-detection layer; MimeType/FileExtension primitives already exist in @beep/schema, so this extends file-processing-capability's detection layer for heterogeneous attorney/court files where extensions lie.
cautions: doctor is BSD-2 (permissive, attribution)
Magika is an ML model dependency - gate behind the existing strategy boundary

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Encoding cascade + mojibake repair for legacy court TXT/HTML/PDF
rationale: The pdfFactory/ca9 mojibake substitution table + 'e absent => corrupt' detector is a court-specific encoding-repair pass extending the file-processing extraction layer; a domain artifact for real Ninth-Circuit opinions, recorded as a reference remediation map.
cautions: doctor is BSD-2 (permissive, attribution)
court-producer-specific (pdffactory) - keep as an opt-in repair pass, not a default transform

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Encoding cascade + mojibake repair for legacy court TXT/HTML/PDF
rationale: The ordered encoding cascade (utf-8 -> ISO8859 -> cp1252 -> latin-1) for legacy Windows-produced WPD/DOC court text extends the file-processing extraction text-decoding layer; reusable decode strategy for old filings, sibling to the mojibake pass.
cautions: doctor is BSD-2 (permissive, attribution)
encoding cascade must record which encoding succeeded for provenance

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Declarative gov-data driver base: auth + retry + Retry-After + Redacted
rationale: AbortController timeout + URLSearchParams passthrough + API-key log redaction is exactly the Effect HttpClient driver-wrapper capability the repo already owns in the LLM drivers (Redacted secret handling, typed timeout/error); catalog gapStatus=dup. Reference-only, no packet opens.
cautions: beep expresses timeouts/errors via Effect typed errors, not try/catch + AbortController
the shared-base extraction itself is tracked by TalentScore#7/us-gov-open-data-mcp#2, not this dup

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Auth-analysis domain model (TokenInfo / TokenLifecycle / AuthEndpoint)
rationale: TokenInfo/TokenLifecycle/AuthenticationEndpoint is a normalized driver auth-descriptor vocabulary feeding the per-driver env-auth matrix in gov-legal-data-driver-codegen; no such shared auth descriptor exists. Cross-links mcp-auth-gated-registration which consumes the descriptor for credential-keyed tool registration.
cautions: harvest-mcp license is unknown/flagged - reimplement the schema shape in effect-Schema, do not copy
study-grade: a vocabulary, not a build target

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Layout-aware PDF extraction + OCR-need heuristic + quality gate
rationale: Extension-switched PDF/DOCX/image ingestion with text-extraction-then-rasterize-to-PNG-then-OCR fallback (and 'empty digital text => OCR' guard) is the text-vs-OCR fallback strategy the file-processing goal defers; extends its OCR strategy boundary for scanned office-action/patent PDFs.
cautions: lawyergpt license is unknown - reimplement the fallback strategy, do not copy Go/gosseract code
panic-isolation maps to Effect defect-vs-failure boundaries, not deferred recover()

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Hybrid BM25 + dense-vector retrieval & pgvector HNSW projection
rationale: 768-dim pgvector column + HNSW vector_cosine_ops index + thresholded (gt 0.25) top-k cosine retrieval is the near-drop-in vector-projection schema for the net-new rag-retrieval-projection packet; beep keeps vectors as rebuildable projection with provenance spans attached.
cautions: lawyergpt is unknown-license and pins Gemini embeddings (embedding-001/text-embedding-004 shut down 2025-2026) - do NOT pin Gemini; beep is provider-neutral/local-first (local ONNX)
pgvector dimension + HNSW must match the chosen local embedding output dimension

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Config-driven legal-corpus scraper (CSS selector + batched POST)
rationale: Env-driven URL list + configurable goquery CSS selector + batched POST is a jurisdiction-specific case-law harvesting variant of the gov/legal data-source driver family; coordinates with the existing firecrawl scraping driver rather than reinventing fetch, recorded as a study reference (serendipitous, P3).
cautions: lawyergpt license is unknown - reimplement the selector/batching pattern, do not copy
HTML-scrape source (vs structured API) - keep it a distinct strategy from the API drivers; firecrawl may subsume the fetch layer

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Layout-aware PDF extraction + OCR-need heuristic + quality gate
rationale: pdfjs page-by-page extraction + assessTextQuality (word-count / real-letter-ratio gibberish detection / repeated-char regex) is the PDF-to-text quality gate that decides OCR-need and refuses low-quality input before it becomes a candidate; extends the file-processing quality-gate layer.
cautions: legalmind-ai license is unknown - reimplement the heuristic, do not copy
CJK-specific letter-ratio regex must generalize to Latin legal text

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Token-bucket rate limiter (per-source, FIFO-fair) as an Effect primitive
rationale: The per-tier token-bucket map + per-API-key header fetcher is the shared driver rate-limit/auth kernel needed across uspto + all gov drivers; reimplemented as an Effect Layer in the gov-legal-data-driver-codegen shared base (the USPTO-tier specifics belong to uspto-patent-driver-depth).
cautions: mcp-uspto is MIT (portable) - reimplement the bucket as an Effect primitive, not raw setInterval
do NOT carry over the patentsview tier (sunset 301->ODP); odp/tsdr only

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> PatentsView / USPTO query-DSL builder + friendly->API field mapping
rationale: The _or/_and/_text_any/_begins/_gte/_lte query DSL + field-selection/pagination/sort + CPC filtering is the structured search surface the hand-rolled uspto driver lacks (searchApplications only forwards a raw ?q=); extends packages/drivers/uspto via the uspto-patent-driver-depth packet, built against ODP.
cautions: PatentsView is mid-sunset (api.patentsview.org 301->data.uspto.gov/odp) - target the DSL at ODP, never reintroduce PatentsView
CPC filtering feeds WIPO-IPC/CPC taxonomy work (ip-law-knowledge-graph)

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> CourtListener citation parser + bulk-first/local-cache fallback
rationale: The CourtListener V4 client with bulk-data-first then live-API fallback (and 'bulk+api' source-tag merge) is the implementation for the bare courtlistener skeleton, owned by gov-legal-data-driver-codegen; the {volume,reporter,page} citation regex parser is downstream citation-grounding work (secondary).
cautions: mike is unknown/AGPL-adjacent license - reimplement the cache-then-API architecture and parser from spec, do not copy
keep citation parsing in citation-grounding-hallucination-guard, not duplicated in the driver

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> New driver: EPO OPS (OAuth2 token cache + INPADOC family/legal-status)
rationale: EPO Open Patent Services is a complete international patent-family + legal-status source with no driver anywhere in the repo (packages/drivers/epo does NOT exist = fully net-new); its OAuth2 client-credentials token cache + black-traffic-light throttle + force-array XML parsing is built under the uspto-patent-driver-depth wedge as a new sibling driver.
cautions: packages/drivers/epo is net-new (greenfield package); anchor packet is packages/drivers/uspto per the patent-driver-depth wedge
EPO OPS requires OAuth2 client-credentials secrets - out of scope for offline verification; needs secrets handling
patents-mcp-server is MIT (portable) - reimplement in Effect/effect-Schema

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> New driver: Google Patents BigQuery (cost-gated full-text + claims extraction)
rationale: Mandatory dryRun-before-execute cost gating + EXISTS/UNNEST over localized title/abstract structs + citation-network CTE is the cost-governance pattern for a net-new Google Patents BigQuery driver (no BigQuery code anywhere in repo), built under the uspto-patent-driver-depth wedge.
cautions: packages/drivers/google-patents is net-new (greenfield); anchor packet is packages/drivers/uspto
BigQuery requires GCP credentials + cost controls ($5/TB) - secrets + budget governance out of offline scope
patents-mcp-server is MIT

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> USPTO ppubs (Public Search) session handshake + request schema
rationale: The reverse-engineered ppubs.uspto.gov handshake (seed cookies -> POST session with X-Access-Token:null -> caseId/token, 403-refresh, 429-backoff via x-rate-limit-retry-after-seconds) adds full-text search the ODP API does not cover; extends the hand-rolled uspto driver as a new ppubs service module.
cautions: ppubs is an undocumented/reverse-engineered endpoint - brittle, version against breakage
patents-mcp is MIT (portable); reimplement handshake/retry as Effect with typed errors
distinct from ODP (data.uspto.gov) - this is the ppubs full-text surface, not File Wrapper

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> USPTO ppubs (Public Search) session handshake + request schema
rationale: The exact ppubs searchWithBeFamily JSON wire format (familyIdFirstPreferred US-PGPUB>USPAT>FPRS, per-database filters, plurals/britishEquivalents, .pn. brs syntax) is hard-to-discover and modeled as a PpubsSearchRequest effect-Schema in the uspto driver; sibling to patents-mcp#1.
cautions: patents-mcp is MIT (portable)
wire format is tied to the undocumented ppubs endpoint - schema-first decode with typed validation

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> New driver: Google Patents BigQuery (cost-gated full-text + claims extraction)
rationale: Injection-safe parameterized BigQuery SQL over patents-public-data (full-text/inventor/assignee/CPC + nested claims_localized/description_localized extraction, 90M+ pubs) is the non-USPTO global prior-art source feeding PriorArtReference/CandidateClaim ingestion; same net-new google-patents BigQuery driver as patents-mcp-server#10.
cautions: packages/drivers/google-patents is net-new (greenfield); coordinate with patents-mcp-server#10 to build one BigQuery driver
claims/descriptions are NESTED in publications (no separate tables)
patents-mcp is MIT

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> USPTO error-shape normalization (tagged-error taxonomy)
rationale: The ApiError factory enumerating USPTO's varying error JSON variants (error/message/errorCode/errorDetails) is the input spec for extending the existing packages/drivers/uspto/src/Uspto.errors.ts tagged-error taxonomy; reference-grade normalization, owned by uspto-patent-driver-depth.
cautions: beep uses Effect tagged errors, not dict shapes - model the field variants as typed errors
Uspto.errors.ts already exists - extend it, do not create a parallel error model
patents-mcp is MIT

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Source-authority inference from URL (primary-source taxonomy)
rationale: inferSourceType/isPrimarySource (.gov/.edu/arxiv/sec.gov => primary) is a small source-classification taxonomy for weighting authority in retrieval ranking; lands in rag-retrieval-projection's ranking layer, extended with legal-specific domains (uspto/courtlistener as primary), cross-linking citation grounding.
cautions: research-squad license unverified - reimplement the heuristic, do not copy
extend the taxonomy with .gov/court/uspto legal domains as primary authority

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> CourtListener court-ID -> full court-name lookup table (365 courts)
rationale: seals.json (365 CourtListener court-ID -> canonical name) is a ready-made static enrichment dataset for resolving court identifiers in citations/provenance without an API round-trip; lands in the net-new court-vocabulary-resolver alongside the courts-db dataset (courts-db#6).
cautions: seal-rookery is a Free Law Project asset - confirm dataset license/attribution before vendoring
static lookup table; resolver logic stays in court-vocabulary-resolver

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Declarative gov-data driver base: auth + retry + Retry-After + Redacted
rationale: createClient(config) with declarative auth (query/header/body + envParams) + disk TTL cache + exponential-backoff-with-jitter honoring Retry-After + per-source rate limit is the exact multi-provider resilient-fetch base the bare gov drivers need; the architectural anchor for the gov-legal-data-driver-codegen shared Effect HTTP layer.
cautions: us-gov-open-data-mcp license is unverified - reimplement the auth-config shape + Retry-After parsing in Effect, do not copy
port the config-object pattern onto Effect HttpApi/effect-Schema, not subclassing

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Token-bucket rate limiter (per-source, FIFO-fair) as an Effect primitive
rationale: The dependency-free TokenBucket (time-refill, fast-path, FIFO queue drained in one pass to avoid thundering-herd, per-source perSecond/burst) is the cleanest reference for the shared rate-limit Effect primitive in gov-legal-data-driver-codegen; sibling to mcp-uspto#1's per-tier map.
cautions: us-gov-open-data-mcp license is unverified - reimplement as an Effect primitive (Semaphore/Schedule), do not copy
consolidate with mcp-uspto#1 into one shared rate-limit utility

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Per-spec driver registration one-liner (orval config)
rationale: The one-line per-spec config (spec file + base URL) is the near-zero-boilerplate driver registration convention the gov-legal-data-driver-codegen pipeline wants; reference for the registration surface, reimplemented on beep's runpod/acp openapi.json+generate.ts Effect codegen precedent (not Orval).
cautions: us-legal-tools is MIT but the value is the codegen PIPELINE/PATTERN, not the Orval/axios/Zod output - port the architecture onto Effect/HttpApi, do not adopt Orval
ecfr is a bare skeleton; registration convention applies across all four gov skeletons

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> CourtListener REST v4 token-auth client
rationale: The exact CourtListener auth shape (Authorization: Token <env COURTLISTENER_API_TOKEN>, User-Agent, baseURL /api/rest/v4, timeout) is the concrete config/service wiring for the bare courtlistener skeleton; corrects the common Bearer mistake (DRF TokenAuthentication uses 'Token ' prefix).
cautions: us-legal-tools is MIT - reuse the env/header convention, reimplement on Effect HttpClient
CourtListener V4 ENFORCES auth (anonymous=401); header is literally 'Authorization: Token <key>', target /api/rest/v4 (V3 legacy), do NOT generate deprecated SCOTUS-viz endpoints

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> PatentsView / USPTO query-DSL builder + friendly->API field mapping
rationale: buildQuery() composing _text_any over title+abstract + nested assignees/inventors field paths + _gte/_lte date clauses under _and is the query-construction layer the raw-?q= uspto driver lacks; extends packages/drivers/uspto search surface (translating attorney prior-art intent to structured queries), built against ODP.
cautions: PatentsView v1 sunset (api.patentsview.org 301->ODP) - build the DSL against data.uspto.gov/odp, never PatentsView
uspto-patents-mcp is MIT; reimplement the builder natively in Effect

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> PatentsView v1 sunset -> ODP migration guard
rationale: The sunset detector (301-to-HTML => actionable migration error, not cryptic JSON parse fail) is operational intelligence encoded as a UsptoEndpointSunset tagged error + ODP endpoint selection in the existing Uspto.errors.ts; confirms the driver must target data.uspto.gov/odp from the start.
cautions: the hand-rolled uspto driver already targets ODP (api/v1/patent/applications) - this guard hardens against accidental PatentsView reintroduction
PatentSearch->ODP migration 2026-03-20 with mandatory key reissue; legacy Developer Hub decommissioned 2026-06-05
uspto-patents-mcp is MIT

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Response cache: KV memoize with TTL jitter + stable cache-key
rationale: KvCache.memoize (TTL + up-to-10% jitter to avoid stampede, 1.5x expiration) + stableKey() (sorted-key deterministic serialization) is the shared driver response-cache utility for deterministically caching upstream USPTO/CourtListener responses; lands in the gov-legal-data-driver-codegen shared base (with us-gov-open-data-mcp#2's disk cache).
cautions: uspto-patents-mcp is MIT - reimplement memoize as an Effect cache layer
consolidate with the us-gov-open-data-mcp#2 disk-TTL cache into one cache utility

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> Lucene query-term escaping with documented safe/unsafe char policy
rationale: escape_lucene_query_term (escapes metachars but deliberately leaves colon/quotes/brackets/dash/wildcards with documented rationale + 1000-char DoS cap) is the injection-safe escaping the uspto search DSL needs; the rationale comments are the gold. Extends packages/drivers/uspto query builder (also reusable for semantic-web full-text).
cautions: uspto_pfw_mcp is MIT (portable)
preserve the documented safe/unsafe-char policy rationale verbatim in comments when reimplementing

goldSynthesisSection: Legal/court/patent data ingestion -> PatentsView / USPTO query-DSL builder + friendly->API field mapping
rationale: map_query_field_names (friendly patentNumber: -> nested applicationMetaData.patentNumber:, pass through already-qualified) gives attorney-facing query vocabulary while targeting the real ODP File-Wrapper schema; the friendly->API field-mapping layer of the uspto-patent-driver-depth search surface.
cautions: uspto_pfw_mcp is MIT (portable)
map to ODP File-Wrapper field paths (applicationMetaData.*), not legacy PatentsView fields
